digest.component_or_area: project infrastructure serial auto-lander (Gerrit + GitHub Actions)
propositions: Parallel agent sessions race to land on FFO main, forcing manual rebase-and-retry and burning redundant CI runs and agent attention.
Build a serial auto-lander service that rebases the front Autosubmit change/chain to tip, awaits a fresh Verified, and ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA with zero further agent attention.

Provide a single project land command returning one typed terminal outcome (merged, needs_rebase, ci_failed, review_failed) with the bot as the single source of truth, so agents never correlate Gerrit votes/labels themselves.
Preserve the strong gate: FFO plus strict Verified, two-vote gate never weakened, nothing lands whose to-be-merged tree wasn't CI-tested, with GerriScary/CVE-2025-1568 security posture audited.
Keep all Gerrit-specific landing logic in infra/ and project tooling, adding none to platform-agnostic src/rebar core (may import rebar only for ticket ops).
Defer Phase-2 cross-stack batching (rejected by design spike) to a bounded-speculation idea, never partial-landing or evicting a change from within a stack.
